texto: The point is, there is no point." Philip spoke up surprisingly. "No one here gets out alive. And over a sufficient period of time, all choices tend to normalize on a curve of random distribution.""You mean if you wait long enough, nothing happens?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Rosemary Edghill que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “The point is, there is no point." Philip spoke up surprisingly. "No one here gets out alive. And …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, fate, meaning-of-life, com foco em termino e meaning-of-life.

frase_original: The point is, there is no point." Philip spoke up surprisingly. "No one here gets out alive. And over a sufficient period of time, all choices tend to normalize on a curve of random distribution.""You mean if you wait long enough, nothing happens?

texto: Two hundred generations of European Jews. All gone, just as if they'd never been. It was the first time it was really real for me--just as if I were standing at the top of a ladder and somebody yanked the ladder away--and I was still standing there, only now it was *possible* to fall, because all my connections had been cut away, and there I was looking down into empty space, thinking about how I'd come this close to just not existing at all.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Rosemary Edghill: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Two hundred generations of European Jews. All gone, just as if they'd never been. It was the firs…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, fantasy, holocaust pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de holocaust.
